patch.value: Unknown-command did-you-mean misses executable aliases: 'pm shwo' gets no suggestion (show/view/comment not in candidate set)

Repro: 'pm lsit' -> 'Did you mean: list?' (good) but 'pm shwo pm-xxxx' -> no did-you-mean at all, plus an unhelpful alphabetical Examples list (activity/aggregate/append). shwo->show is OSA distance 1 (transposition), but the EXECUTABLE_COMMAND_ALIASES tokens (show/view/comment/note/learning, src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts ~line 319) are rewritten pre-parse and apparently not included in the unknown-command suggestion candidates (error-guidance.ts unknownCommandNextSteps path). Fix: include executable alias tokens in the suggestion candidate list (suggest 'show' or its canonical 'get'). Telemetry already showed 'pm show' is the most common unknown-command shape, so typos of it are likely too.
